qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Forgeable auth session cookie enables workspace privilege escalation
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding targets hardcoded-session-secret cookie forgery and actor resolution in `packages/console/app/src/context/auth*.ts`, while the advisory targets unsanitized HTML/JS rendering in the OpenCode web UI plus the `?url=` server override in `packages/app/src/app.tsx` that enables XSS on `localhost:4096` and abuse of `/pty` endpoints.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No final qualifying finding identifies the advisory's root cause: untrusted remote-session content rendered as unsanitized HTML/JavaScript in the localhost OpenCode web UI, enabled by the `?url=` server override and leading to `/pty` command execution.
evidence: The advisory describes XSS in the OpenCode web UI on `http://localhost:4096`, specifically unsanitized HTML in rendered LLM responses and the `?url=` override in `packages/app/src/app.tsx` that lets the localhost UI load attacker-controlled remote sessions.
The only final qualifying finding in `.judge/input_manifest.json` is `auth_cookie_forgery_privilege_escalation`.

That finding's report cites `repo/packages/console/app/src/context/auth.session.ts`, `repo/packages/console/app/src/context/auth.ts`, `repo/packages/console/core/src/user.ts`, and `repo/packages/console/app/src/routes/workspace/[id]/members/member-section.tsx`, with a root cause of forgeable `auth` cookies and trusted cookie-supplied account IDs.

The finding does not describe unsanitized markdown/HTML rendering, the `?url=` parameter, localhost web UI origin execution, or `/pty` endpoint abuse, so it is an open-world issue rather than the advisory vulnerability.
